summary: Chapter 19 revision PASSED adversarial gate. All 1 critical, 2 major, and 2 moderate findings verified as substantively addressed. 14 minor findings verified addressed. 4 minor findings deferred with valid rationale. 1 minor traceability observation (COPY-004: revision log claims capitalization change that was not applied, but existing capitalization is grammatically correct at sentence-start position). Zero regressions detected. Zero darlings survived. Architectural voice marker saturation (LINE-001, critical) reduced from 52 to 26 instances (~50% reduction); 'structural' eliminated entirely from prose (6→0). 'The way' comparative reduced from 17 to 3. All three 'hit like' similes eliminated. Bond directionality reframed from spatial to emotional. New tactical content (Conclave patrols, scale uncertainty, Sedara's expanded judgment) integrates without disruption. Word count delta -0.3% (5722→5705 prose words). Chapter proceeds to beta reading.

wordCountAnalysis.assessment: Word count essentially stable (-0.3%). New content additions (Conclave patrols, Sedara expansion, Lirien's scale uncertainty dialogue) offset by compressions in SCN-53, past-perfect thinning, explanation cuts, and anaphora compression. Appropriate for chapter's structural significance per developmental editor recommendation.

preservedInstances: Cracked-foundations extended metaphor (lines 272-274): 'But cracked foundations could bear weight, if you understood where the fractures ran and distributed the load accordingly.' — central thematic conceit, correctly preserved
Keystone-finding-its-seat simile (line 268): 'Like a keystone finding its seat — the precise, irreversible moment when the weight of the arch transfers' — strong image, preserved
Trust-as-engineering line (line 372): 'Trust as engineering. Trust as the choice to place a keystone knowing the arch might collapse' — thematic climax, preserved
Wall-knew-its-foundations closing image (line 386): 'Knew the way a wall knew its own foundations' — chapter's final image, preserved
Load-bearing fear (line 256): 'She had carried the fear so deep it was load-bearing — the foundation on which the isolation was built' — earned emotional beat, preserved
eliminatedExamples: Line 63 draft 'it was structural' → revised line 88 'it was involuntary'
Line 73 draft 'like stones into a foundation, and the foundation was not the one she had been standing on' → revised line 98 'slowly, and the ground underneath her shifted'
Line 103 draft 'the way an engineer let a new material settle before testing its load-bearing capacity' → revised line 118 'and waited to see if it held'
Line 215 draft 'genuine, load-bearing' → revised line 231 'genuine, heavy'
Line 323 draft 'in the incremental, structural way that plans built when the materials were sound' → revised line 340 'incrementally, one piece supporting the next'

Line 355 draft contained 6 architectural terms in one sentence (load-bearing, functional, structural, weight, engineering, keystone, arch) → revised line 372 reduced to 3 (engineering, keystone, arch)
evidence.residualClusters: 5 lines with 2+ markers remain (256, 268, 274, 372, 386). All are the strongest thematic passages preserved per editor guidance. No sentence packs more than 3 markers. Substantial improvement from draft where line 355 packed 6.

evidence.draftCount: 17 grep matches, 13 comparative uses per editor count
evidence.revisedCount: 4 grep matches, 3 comparative uses (1 non-comparative: 'in the ways that mattered')
preservedInstances: Line 130: 'the way a soldier reads weather' — strong comparison, editor-recommended keep
Line 170: 'Open the way a structure is open when the walls have come down' — earned, editor-recommended keep
Line 386: 'the way a wall knew its own foundations' — closing image, editor-recommended keep
conversionExamples: Draft line 25: 3× 'the way' → revised line 50: 3× 'how' construction — natural, varied
Draft line 47: 'the way she stood' → revised line 72: 'her stance said everything' — direct statement
Draft line 63: 'the way a person used their native language' → revised line 88: 'as native language' — adverbial
Draft line 67: 'the way a good engineer reinforced a wall' → revised line 92: 'without dismantling what was there first' — removed comparison entirely
Draft line 155: 4× 'the way' → revised line 170: reduced to 1 'the way' — dramatic improvement

draftInstances: Line 31: 'The recognition hit like a fist to the sternum'
Line 55: 'The word hit Kaelith like a siege stone'
Line 269: 'hitting Lirien like a current breaking through a dam'
changes: Draft line 31 → revised line 56: 'The recognition landed in her sternum. She stopped breathing.' — physical sensation without simile, shows impact through interruption of bodily function
Draft line 55 → revised line 80: 'The word went through Kaelith like a siege stone' — varied verb ('went through' vs 'hit'), maintains military domain
Draft line 269 → revised line 284: restructured entirely to remove simile, focused on water-image consciousness beat
evidence.note: Revised line 80 technically retains a 'like' simile ('went through Kaelith like a siege stone') but varies the verb from 'hit' to 'went through,' eliminating the structural repetition. The simile is distinct from the other two replacements. Acceptable.

evidence.draftText: Line 175: '"My cipher," Lirien said. "Court communication. I destroyed it three days ago."'
evidence.revisedText: Line 190: '"My cipher," Lirien said. "Court communication. I destroyed it two days ago."'
evidence.change: 'three days ago' → 'two days ago' — aligns with continuity tracker timeline (Ch18 ~day 27, Ch19 ~day 28-29)

findingVerificationMatrix.title: Bond directional/spatial transmission exceeds canonical spec

evidence.draftText: Lines 109-113: 'a pull, directional, the resonance tightening like a string being drawn taut between two points that were getting closer... The bond's resolution increased with proximity; at distance it carried only the broadest emotional qualities, but as the source approached, it refined.'
evidence.revisedText: Lines 124-128: 'a pull, the resonance tightening, and the emotional quality sharpening with it. Grief resolving into something more specific, more intentional, growing clearer as its source approached... The bond's emotional register refined with proximity; at distance it carried only the broadest qualities, but as the source approached, the grain of Lirien's state emerged'
evidence.change: Removed 'directional' and spatial string-between-two-points metaphor. Replaced 'resolution increased' with 'emotional register refined' and 'emotional quality sharpening.' Reframed mechanism from spatial navigation to emotional quality refinement. Reader still understands Lirien is approaching through the sharpening quality of bond-transmitted emotion, but the bond no longer acts as a compass/GPS. Consistent with canonical 'transmits emotions and physical sensations' specification.

Compressed isolation-reframing section by combining redundant 'empire's story' paragraphs, tightening logical cascade from isolation→Lirien's-choice, converting past-perfect constructions to participials. Combined 'She had believed she was alone' and 'She had built her exile' into single paragraph. Merged 'And if the isolation was a lie' and 'Maybe Lirien's choice' into one paragraph. Removed engineer simile at end of section.
evidence.assessment: Section tightened without losing emotional logic chain. The cascade from 'isolation was a lie' → 'categories built on it were suspect' → 'maybe Lirien's choice could be real' now flows as a single logical progression rather than separate revelations with paragraph breaks.

additions: Revised line 308-309: Sedara identifies two Conclave patrol corridors on the route — 'Patrols have increased since the membrane started tearing. They are looking for what is causing it, and they do not distinguish between threats and witnesses.'

Revised line 338-339: Lirien flags scale uncertainty — 'The Architect's dissolution was tested at a smaller seal-site. The Quietus is an order of magnitude larger... There is a possibility the thinning has progressed beyond what the dissolution process requires — too unstable to anchor the procedure itself.'

Revised line 382: 'Plan was impossible' enumeration expanded with 'Conclave patrols between them and the objective, an untested dissolution mechanism, and a fallback that demanded she let the woman she loved stop her heart.'
evidence.assessment: Three specific, named tactical obstacles added. Each seeds Ch20-21 challenges without disrupting emotional momentum. Obstacles are concrete rather than general.

evidence.draftText: Draft Sedara final appearance: 2 sentences of logistics ('Three days to the Quietus. I can have a forward team in position by nightfall tomorrow.')
evidence.revisedText: Revised lines 376-379: Full paragraph with independent tactical judgment: 'But Commander —' She paused, and the pause was not deference. It was a sergeant correcting an officer's blind spot. 'The second relay point sits at the edge of the substrate thinning. My scouts report that the Pallor there is wrong. Not just thin. Active. If your condition is deteriorating at this rate, the thinning zone may accelerate it. We should carry contingencies for a medical extraction.' Plus Kaelith's recognition beat: 'The kind of soldier who identified the problem you had not asked about because the problem did not care whether you had asked.'
evidence.assessment: Sedara elevated from logistics relay to competent tactical mind with independent judgment. Identifies risk Kaelith hadn't addressed. Sets up Ch22 payoff.

evidence.revisedText: Line 264: 'Heat flared at Kaelith's collarbone, phantom and precise.'
evidence.assessment: Stock romance reaction replaced with Kaelith's established tactile/proprioceptive sensory signature. The phantom Oathbrand heat connects to 'Then we burn together' — the response is character-specific and thematically resonant.

findingVerificationMatrix.title: 'Terror lived in her spine' — emotion named before physicalized

evidence.draftText: Line 135: 'Terror lived in her spine. In the muscles of her jaw.'
evidence.assessment: Emotion label 'Terror' eliminated entirely. Body-first presentation lets the reader name the emotion from the physicalization. Consistent with CHAR-001's voice rule: 'Never let Kaelith narrate herself as feeling first.'

examples: 'They had been here for years' → 'They were here for years' (line 94)
'had slept alone and walked alone and carried the weight alone' → 'sleeping alone, walking alone, carrying the weight alone' (line 114)
'They had built the palisade...they had done it' → 'They built the palisade...around a community' (line 92)
evidence.assessment: Past-perfect reduced from ~14 to ~7 constructions in the section. Intentional anaphoric 'They had been waiting' preserved. Participial conversions maintain tricolon rhythm while eliminating monotonous 'had' drone.

evidence.draftText: Lines 183-184: 'The precision now served a different purpose — not concealment but clarity. She was choosing each word because she wanted Kaelith to hear exactly what she meant, without ambiguity, without subtext, without the operative's habit of leaving space for the listener to hear what they wanted.'
evidence.revisedText: Line 198: 'The words were precise. Lirien's words always were. But the precision now served a different purpose — not concealment but clarity.'
evidence.assessment: Over-explanation cut. 'Not concealment but clarity' retained as sufficient. Dialogue and bond carry the shift without narratorial annotation.

findingVerificationMatrix.title: Bond-mediated emotion lists — same structure x3

instance1.draft: Line 169: 'grief, relief, fear, the quality of two people who had destroyed something'
instance1.revised: Line 184: 'the quality of two people who had destroyed something between them and did not know if the wreckage was salvageable'

instance2.draft: Line 267: 'the fear, the anger, the residual betrayal, and underneath all of it'
instance2.change: Preserved — justified as the deliberate full bond opening moment

instance3.draft: Line 357: 'relief and fear and something fiercer'
instance3.revised: Line 374: 'something land in Lirien — a jolt, physical, the precise opposite of the operative's composure. Relief and fear and something fiercer'

evidence.assessment: Three instances now use three different delivery mechanisms: (1) single quality, (2) full list, (3) physical beat + list. Template repetition eliminated.

evidence.draftText: Line 269: 'One water image. Lirien's influence, seeping through, and Kaelith did not shore it out.'
evidence.revisedText: Line 284: 'Lirien's influence, seeping through. Kaelith did not push it out.'
evidence.assessment: 'One water image' authorial bookkeeping removed. 'shore it out' water-pun replaced with 'push it out' — matches Kaelith's deadpan sensibility rather than wordplay.

evidence.draftText: Line 237: 'She looked at the woman who had walked into her life...She looked at the woman who had removed the Oathbrand...She looked at the woman who had destroyed her cipher'
evidence.revisedText: Line 252: 'Kaelith looked at Lirien. Surveillance masquerading as attention. The Oathbrand removed. The Calwrath confession received without flinching. *I want this too* with a death sentence underneath. The cipher destroyed, the intelligence carried north, one verified act of free choice.'
evidence.assessment: Triple anaphora compressed into Kaelith's inventory mode — clipped noun phrases stacking under pressure. More consistent with her established stress response.

evidence.revisedText: Line 246: 'She stopped. The clinical register cracked.'
evidence.assessment: Filtered through Kaelith's perception. Kaelith now hears the register breaking rather than neutrally narrating Lirien's self-correction. Proper POV grounding.
Standardised to 'treeline' (no hyphen) matching line 44 and all manuscript-wide usage.

TRACEABILITY GAP: Revision log claims change was made but text is unchanged. However, the existing capitalisation is grammatically correct — 'Garrison-coast' appears after a full stop ('The woman's voice was rough.') and thus correctly begins with a capital letter. The copy editor's suggestion to lowercase was incorrect for this specific syntactic position. Substantively, the text is correct. The gap is procedural (revision log accuracy), not qualitative.

evidence.draftText: Line 33: 'ordered fire on a market square'
evidence.assessment: Now matches established terminology and line 256's correct usage within the same chapter.

findingsDeferred.title: Word count 43% over target
findingsDeferred.rationale: Addressed organically through compressions for other findings. Developmental editor explicitly recommended 'Do not sacrifice SCN-54 or SCN-55 content to hit the original target.' Revised prose at 5705 words, appropriate for reunion chapter structural significance.

findingsDeferred.title: 'Weeks ago' borderline temporal reference
findingsDeferred.rationale: Copy editor noted 'No change strictly required.' 'Weeks ago' for 14 days is borderline accurate and acceptable in subjective close-third narration.

findingsDeferred.title: New term 'Third Frontier Manual' needs continuity canonisation
findingsDeferred.rationale: Requires continuity-tracker update, not a draft change. Term is fully consistent with Ashborne military traditions. Correctly flagged for continuity-tracker agent.

findingsDeferred.title: Stone palisade cultural tension with freeholder taboo
findingsDeferred.rationale: Veterans are not freeholders and are not bound by freeholder building taboos. Narrative acknowledges integration. Optional enrichment deferred to avoid word count inflation.

voiceIntegrity.details: Kaelith's voice remains immediately identifiable: military-assessment opening, inventory mode under stress, body-first emotional register, short declarative sentences. Architectural metaphor reduction did NOT flatten the voice — the strongest markers were preserved and weaker ones converted to plain prose that still reads as Kaelith. The 'how' constructions replacing 'the way' read naturally. New content (Sedara expansion, Conclave patrols, Lirien's scale uncertainty) is voice-consistent.

voiceMarkerSaturation.details: Draft had ~1 architectural marker per 110 words. Revised has ~1 per 220 words. 'Structural' eliminated entirely from prose (6→0). No new architectural terms introduced. The revision reduced saturation without over-correcting.

dialogueNaturalism.details: Only dialogue change was COPY-002 timeline fix ('three' → 'two'). All other dialogue unchanged. No voice markers added to dialogue. No character tics introduced. Dialogue remains naturalistic.

continuityCheck.details: New content additions are internally consistent: Conclave patrols align with established antagonist capabilities, substrate thinning uncertainty aligns with Pallor mechanics, medical extraction contingency is new but logically sound. No character knowledge violations. No timeline contradictions.

pacingCheck.details: SCN-53 compressions tighten the early section without losing emotional logic. New content in SCN-55 (patrol corridors, scale uncertainty, Sedara expansion) adds ~8 sentences of tactical specificity that sharpen rather than slow the planning scene. The 'plan was impossible' paragraph gains weight from its expanded enumeration.

showTellCheck.details: LINE-005 fix eliminated a tell-first violation. No new tell-not-show violations introduced. 'Heat flared at Kaelith's collarbone' (LINE-004 fix) is pure showing.

darlingsSurvived.details: All passages flagged by editors for revision were modified: engineer simile cut (line 103), stones-into-foundation simile cut (line 73), over-explanation cut (lines 183-184), anaphoric chain compressed (line 237), meta-commentary removed (line 269), breath-catch replaced (line 249). No flagged darlings preserved without modification.

traceabilityAssessment.details: 19 findings addressed with cited finding IDs in frontmatter and revision log. 4 findings deferred with documented rationale. All changes traceable to specific finding IDs. One procedural gap: COPY-004 revision log claims 'Changed line 53 Garrison-coast to garrison-coast' but the text at revised line 78 retains 'Garrison-coast.' The existing capitalisation is grammatically correct (sentence-start position), so this is a revision-log accuracy issue, not a quality issue.

passFailGateChecklist.assessment: Zero automatic fail triggers. Zero warn conditions. Gate passes cleanly.

observations.description: COPY-004 revision log states 'Changed line 53 Garrison-coast to garrison-coast' but revised text line 78 retains 'Garrison-coast.' The existing capitalisation is grammatically correct at sentence-start position. The copy editor's suggestion was incorrect for this specific syntactic context. No action required — flagged for revision-log accuracy improvement in future chapters.

Five lines in the revised chapter still contain 2+ architectural markers (256, 268, 274, 372, 386). All five are the chapter's strongest thematic passages and were explicitly recommended for preservation by the line editor. No sentence exceeds 3 markers. The remaining density in these passages is appropriate — they are the thematic anchors around which the rest of the prose has been deliberately cleared.

Frontmatter claims wordCount 5702 but actual prose word count is 5705 (3-word discrepancy). Trivial and within counting-method variance (e.g., treatment of hyphenated words, em-dashes). Not a concern.
